tool_input.summary: No PDS dataset directly provides a single ‘downtail distance of statistical separation’ for Saturn reconnection events; that value comes from a specific published statistical study and must be derived from event identification + spacecraft position. To reproduce/compute it, use Cassini MAG (field signatures of reconnection, plasmoids, dipolarizations) together with Cassini plasma/particle instruments (CAPS/MIMI/RPWS) and SPICE kernels for downtail distance (e.g., -X in KSM/KSO). Below are the most relevant PDS datasets found in the catalog (including PDS4 and PDS3 versions where available).

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset to identify reconnection-related magnetic signatures (plasmoids/flux ropes tailward of the X-line; dipolarizations planetward). Includes 1 s and 1 min averages in Saturn kronographic coordinate systems useful for downtail (-X) distance statistics.

candidates.title: CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1SECAVG V2.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 counterpart for Cassini MAG calibrated summary data at 1-second cadence; useful if you are reproducing older workflows/papers based on PDS3 MAG archives.

candidates.title: CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1MINAVG V2.1
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 1-minute MAG summary product for large statistical surveys (event occurrence vs downtail distance).

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw MAG if you need full-rate/engineering context or want to reproduce calibrations when examining marginal reconnection signatures.

candidates.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived CAPS plasma parameters that can help classify reconnection region (planetward vs tailward flows/heating) when building the event list used in the statistical separation analysis.

candidates.title: CO E J S SW CAPS 3 CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 calibrated CAPS data often used in legacy reconnection/event studies; supports identifying plasma signatures associated with X-line proximity.

candidates.title: CO E J S SW CAPS 2 UNCALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 CAPS uncalibrated archive referenced by some derived CAPS products and older analyses; included for completeness when tracing provenance.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic ion/electron context (MIMI/INCA/LEMMS/CHEMS) can be used to corroborate reconnection-related injections and help distinguish planetward vs tailward event populations.

candidates.title: CO S MIMI 4 INCA CALIB V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 calibrated INCA energetic neutral atom / ion products used in older Saturn tail reconnection studies; can be paired with MAG for event classification.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Plasma density context (sheet crossings, lobe vs plasma sheet) that can improve event selection and interpretation when mapping events relative to the inferred X-line distance.

candidates.title: CASSINI SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels are needed to compute Cassini’s Saturn-centered position in the relevant coordinate system and thus the downtail distance at each event time (e.g., -X in KSM/KSO), which is required to find where the statistical separation occurs.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
At what downtail distance does the statistical separation between events tailward and planetward of the Saturn's reconnection X line?

dataset.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes 1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute averaged data is provided as well.

dataset.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer raw magnetic field data for the entire mission. The bundle includes magnetic-field data from the Fluxgate Magnetometer and the Vector Helium Magnetomer in Vector mode. Data are received in science and housekeeping rates. Additional collections, such as spacecraft attitude, analog, command validation, configuration image, error counter, user-defined engineering data, browse plots, and calibration, are also included.

dataset.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle
dataset.description: This data set consists of all of the fits (both good and bad) that were found by Dr. Robert J. Wilson during the NASA Cassini Data Analysis Program grant (NNX12AG90G). The Cassini CAPS plasma data used in the forward model plasma parameter calculations given here came from volume CO-E/J/S/SW-CAPS-2-UNCALIBRATED-V1.0 in the Planetary Data System (PDS).

dataset.title: Cassini MIMI Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains Cassini Magnetospheric Imaging Instrument (MIMI) mission documentation.
instruments: Magnetospheric Imaging Instrument for Cassini Orbiter
MIMI Low Energy Magnetospheric Measurement Sensor for Cassini Orbiter

dataset.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
dataset.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies. When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density. At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.
instruments: RADIO AND PLASMA WAVE SCIENCE for CO

parts.args: {"summary":"No PDS dataset directly provides a single ‘downtail distance of statistical separation’ for Saturn reconnection events; that value comes from a specific published statistical study and must be derived from event identification + spacecraft position. To reproduce/compute it, use Cassini MAG (field signatures of reconnection, plasmoids, dipolarizations) together with Cassini plasma/particle instruments (CAPS/MIMI/RPWS) and SPICE kernels for downtail distance (e.g., -X in KSM/KSO). Below are the most relevant PDS datasets found in the catalog (including PDS4 and PDS3 versions where available).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal::1.1","title":"Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary dataset to identify reconnection-related magnetic signatures (plasmoids/flux ropes tailward of the X-line; dipolarizations planetward). Includes 1 s and 1 min averages in Saturn kronographic coordinate systems useful for downtail (-X) distance statistics."},{"dataset_id":"CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1SECAVG-V2.0","title":"CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1SECAVG V2.0","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 counterpart for Cassini MAG calibrated summary data at 1-second cadence;
